agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-sale lifecycle. The domain encodes rules around inventory reservation, pricing/discount application, order immutability, and refund eligibility — all standard e-commerce invariants with specific policy choices.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout (with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns)","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient, uncommitted collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Holding items in the cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation and starts the payment window."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-bound hold on inventory units, created when checkout begins and released if payment is not completed within 15 minutes."},{"term":"Reservation Timeout","definition":"The 15-minute window from checkout initiation within which payment must be confirmed; expiry releases the reserved inventory."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A currently effective promotional pricing rule applied to a product. A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that applies a discount. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Discount Mechanism","definition":"Any source of price reduction — either a discount code or a promotional price. Only one mechanism may apply per order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The irreversible state transition after which an order becomes immutable — no modifications allowed."},{"term":"Zero Inventory","definition":"A product whose available stock is zero. Such products remain visible in the catalog but cannot be added to a checkout or purchased."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period following delivery during which a customer may request a refund."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to the cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory is reserved only when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If inventory were reserved at cart-add, phantom holds would corrupt available stock counts system-wide. The separation of cart from reservation is a data-consistency invariant.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: After a customer adds an item to their cart, the product's available inventory count is unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not confirmed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the inventory reservation is released and checkout is abandoned.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy threshold. Operations could adjust this to 10 or 20 minutes without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A reservation created at time T with no payment confirmation is released at T+15 minutes, and the inventory units are returned to available stock."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous active promotions on one product would create ambiguous pricing — the system cannot deterministically resolve which price to display or charge. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any product, COUNT(active promotions) <= 1 at all times. Attempting to activate a second promotion while one is active is rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing. Only one discount mechanism applies per order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing stacking would produce prices below intended minimums and violate pricing integrity. The mutual exclusivity is a hard constraint on order pricing calculation.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: An order's applied discounts list contains at most one entry. If a product has an active promotion and a discount code is applied, only one takes effect."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification would break financial reconciliation, inventory accounting, and fulfillment integrity. This is a state-machine invariant: confirmed orders are immutable.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any attempt to change line items, quantities, shipping address, or pricing on an order in 'payment_confirmed' state (or beyond) is rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling unavailable inventory creates fulfillment failures and oversell conditions. This is a physical constraint — you cannot ship what you do not have.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Attempting to begin checkout with a product whose available inventory is 0 results in an error; the order is not created."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business/legal policy. Management or regulatory changes could extend or shorten it without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A refund request submitted on delivery_date + 31 days is rejected. A request submitted on delivery_date + 30 days is accepted."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"They have not initiated checkout","then":"The product's available inventory count remains unchanged and other customers can purchase those same units"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with items in their cart","when":"All items have sufficient available inventory","then":"Inventory is reserved for those items, reducing available stock by the requested quantities"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has initiated checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment confirmation","then":"The reservation is released, available inventory is restored, and the checkout session is marked as abandoned"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer has initiated checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"Payment is confirmed within 15 minutes","then":"The reservation becomes a permanent allocation, the order is created in confirmed state, and the checkout session is closed"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An administrator attempts to activate a second promotion on the same product","then":"The system rejects the second promotion and returns an error indicating only one active promotion is allowed"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer is checking out with a product that has an active promotional price","when":"The customer also applies a discount code","then":"Only one discount mechanism is applied to the order; the other is excluded"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order has reached payment_confirmed status","when":"Any actor attempts to modify line items, quantities, or pricing","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with zero available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to purchase it (initiate checkout)","then":"The system prevents checkout for that product and informs the customer it is out of stock"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order was delivered on date D","when":"A customer requests a refund on or before D + 30 days","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered on date D","when":"A customer requests a refund after D + 30 days","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message indicating the refund window has expired"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Inventory drops to zero during an active checkout session (another customer buys the last unit)","expected_behavior":"The reservation made at checkout-start holds. If this customer's reservation was successful, it is honored. If inventory was exhausted before their reservation, checkout initiation fails."},{"scenario":"Customer applies a discount code to an order containing a product with an active promotion — which discount wins?","expected_behavior":"AMBIGUOUS — the spec states only one mechanism applies but does not specify priority. Must be resolved: does the promotional price take precedence, does the discount code take precedence, or does the customer choose?"},{"scenario":"Customer abandons checkout at 14:59, then submits payment at 15:01","expected_behavior":"Payment must be rejected. The reservation was released at the 15-minute mark. The customer must restart checkout (and inventory may no longer be available)."},{"scenario":"Product inventory reaches zero between cart-add and checkout-initiation","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation fails for that product. Cart contents are informational only — no guarantee of availability."},{"scenario":"Refund requested for an order that has not yet been delivered","expected_behavior":"AMBIGUOUS — the spec defines the refund window as 30 days from delivery. Pre-delivery refund/cancellation policy is not addressed."},{"scenario":"A promotion is deactivated and a new one activated simultaneously","expected_behavior":"At no point should two promotions be active. Deactivation must complete before activation, or the operation must be atomic ensuring the one-active-promotion invariant holds."},{"scenario":"Partial order refund — some items within 30 days, order contains items delivered on different dates","expected_behavior":"AMBIGUOUS — the spec does not clarify whether the refund window applies per-item (by each item's delivery date) or per-order."},{"scenario":"Customer re-applies a discount code after removing a promoted item from the order","expected_behavior":"If no promotional pricing remains on the order, the discount code should be applicable since the mutual-exclusivity constraint no longer applies."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct inventory reservation prevents overselling (costly fulfillment failures and customer compensation). Single-discount-mechanism rule protects margins. Refund window limits financial exposure.","operational":"15-minute reservation timeout prevents inventory lockup from abandoned checkouts, keeping stock available for other customers. Order immutability post-payment simplifies fulfillment pipeline — no mid-flight changes.","user":"Customers see accurate stock availability. Clear refund window sets expectations. However, the unresolved discount-priority ambiguity could cause checkout confusion or support tickets.","risk":"Primary risk is the undefined discount-priority rule (BR-4 / AC-6): without a deterministic resolution, different code paths could apply different discounts, leading to inconsistent pricing and potential revenue leakage or customer complaints.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected by discount resolution ambiguity — may not understand why a discount code was ignored in favor of a promotion (or vice versa). Also affected by the undefined pre-delivery refund policy."},{"group":"Product/Marketing Team","impact":"Owns promotions — needs clarity on whether promotional pricing always wins over discount codes, as this affects campaign ROI calculations."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability and single-discount rule simplify reconciliation. Refund window bounds liability. But ambiguous discount stacking could cause variance in expected vs actual revenue."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will field complaints about rejected discount codes on promoted products, and about refund requests outside the window or pre-delivery. Needs clear policy to communicate."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Inventory reservation and zero-stock rules prevent oversell, reducing fulfillment exceptions and emergency sourcing."}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The spec is largely well-defined with clear rules for inventory, checkout lifecycle, order immutability, and refund windows. Confidence is capped at 55 because there is a material ambiguity: when both a discount code and a promotional price could apply, the spec does not define which takes priority. Additionally, the refund policy is silent on pre-delivery scenarios and per-item vs per-order granularity.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is not reserved until checkout — clearly stated","15-minute reservation timeout — explicit","One active promotion per product — explicit","Order immutability after payment — explicit","Zero inventory blocks purchase — explicit","30-day refund window from delivery — explicit"],"low_confidence_areas":["Discount mechanism priority when both a code and a promotion apply — spec says one applies but not which one","Pre-delivery refund or cancellation policy — not addressed","Whether refund window is per-item delivery date or per-order delivery date for multi-shipment orders","Whether the 30-day refund window is inclusive or exclusive of the 30th day"],"assumptions":["The 15-minute timeout is measured from the moment checkout is initiated, not from some other event","Inventory reservation is all-or-nothing per checkout — partial reservation behavior is not specified but assumed to fail the entire checkout","Refund window boundary (30 days) is inclusive — request on day 30 is accepted"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When a customer applies a discount code to an order that contains a product with active promotional pricing, the spec states only one discount mechanism applies but does not define which one takes priority. This is a pricing-critical ambiguity that affects revenue, customer experience, and support.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Marketing Team","Finance/Accounting","Customer Support"],"options":["A) Promotional pricing always wins — discount code is rejected with explanation","B) Discount code always wins — promotional price is overridden","C) The greater discount (for the customer) wins automatically","D) The customer is presented a choice at checkout"],"recommendation":"Option A (promotional pricing takes priority). Promotions are strategically planned by the business and priced intentionally. Discount codes are customer-initiated and could undercut campaign goals. Recommend: if a promotion is active, the promotional price applies and the discount code is rejected with a clear message. This is simplest to implement, easiest to explain, and protects marketing intent."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The refund policy specifies '30 days from delivery' but does not address: (1) whether a refund can be requested before delivery (e.g., order cancellation), or (2) for multi-shipment orders, whether the window applies per-item based on individual delivery dates or per-order based on the last delivery date.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Customer Support","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["A) Pre-delivery refunds are allowed as order cancellations under a separate policy","B) No refunds until delivery — customer must wait","C) Per-item delivery date for multi-shipment; per-order for single-shipment"],"recommendation":"Option A for pre-delivery (allow cancellation as a distinct flow) and per-item delivery dates for the refund window. This is the most customer-friendly interpretation and aligns with standard e-commerce practice."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-sale lifecycle. The domain encodes rules around inventory reservation, pricing/discount application, order immutability, and refund eligibility — all standard e-commerce invariants with specific policy choices.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout (with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns)","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definitio

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me classify each business rule and determine if there are any escalations needed.

Looking at the rules:
1. Inventory reserved at checkout, not at cart add
2. 15-minute reservation timeout
3. Max one active promotion per product
4. Discount codes can't combine with promotional pricing
5. Orders immutable after payment confirmed
6. Zero inventory = can't purchase even if listed
7. Refunds within 30 days of delivery

These are all fairly clear. No contradictions, no significant ambiguity. Let me produce the analysis.


Actually, I'm noticing some edge cases that warrant closer examination. The discount mechanism rule is genuinely ambiguous—it says only one applies per order, but doesn't specify which takes priority when both a discount code and promotional pricing could apply. The 15-minute checkout timeout seems clear enough as starting from when checkout begins, but the refund policy raises questions about items still in transit and whether partial refunds are allowed. The discount priority issue is the real ambiguity here that needs escalation.

Now I'm putting together the full JSON response with a confidence level around 55.
